description: Pick your fandom, find K-pop and K-culture words fast, and keep climbing your personal league. Overall #1 wins a Korea trip, and each league winner gets a K-culture box.
rewardText: Overall #1: Korea trip | League #1: K-culture box
termsAgree: I agree to the Terms of Service and prize policy.
prizeLeagueBanner: Each league winner -> K-culture box - Resets at midnight
termsFullText:
Terms of Service

1. Purpose
These terms govern the use of StanBeat (the "Service"), including conditions, procedures, and the rights and obligations of users and operators.

2. Prize Policy
1) Prizes are awarded to top rankers after each season ends.
2) Prizes may be substituted with equivalent K-culture items or rolled over if participation is below 5,000.
3) Korea trip or travel prize issuance may take up to 180 days after winner announcement.
4) Taxes are the winner responsibility.
5) Shipping delays may occur.

3. Heart System
1) One heart is consumed per game play.
2) Maximum 3 hearts can be stored.
3) Hearts do not expire automatically once earned.
4) You can receive 1 free heart when you log in every 6 hours based on UTC time, but you can only store up to 3 hearts.

4. Unofficial Fan Challenge
StanBeat is an unofficial fan challenge and is not affiliated with or endorsed by any artist, label, or entertainment company.

5. Prohibited Actions
Records obtained through automation, macros, or abnormal access will be invalidated and accounts may be permanently suspended.

6. Disclaimer
The service is provided as-is; operators may suspend or modify the service without prior notice.

testimonial1: Picked K-culture Mix and won a K-culture box after topping my league.
testimonial2: I switched to BLACKPINK mode, cleared the board, and shared the challenge in our group chat.
testimonial3: The K-drama and Seoul words got me hooked. I started practicing Korean phrases after playing StanBeat.
testimonial4: My friend sent me the NewJeans challenge link and I kept retrying until I beat their time.
testimonial5: I used TWICE mode during my commute and finally hit league #1.
testimonial6: I joined for aespa, stayed for the league rivalry, and discovered more K-culture keywords.
testimonial7: The Stray Kids challenge went viral in our STAY chat.
testimonial8: I chose LE SSERAFIM mode and practiced every day until my record dropped under 30 seconds.
testimonial9: ATEEZ mode made the game feel like it was made for my fandom.
testimonial10: IVE mode plus the Korea trip prize was enough motivation to keep trying.
testimonial11: SEVENTEEN fans in our chat started comparing clear times.
testimonial12: ENHYPEN mode gave me a reason to invite friends without explaining the game.
testimonial13: K-culture Mix is perfect when you like idols, dramas, food, beauty, and Seoul travel.
testimonial14: I like that the prize is a K-culture box instead of one group only.
testimonial15: The fandom selector makes every share link feel personal.
testimonial16: Started with BTS, then tried ATEEZ and NewJeans. Same record, fresh challenge.
testimonial17: The Seoul trip prize still feels big, but the group themes make the first click easier.
testimonial18: My K-pop friends each picked different fandoms and still competed in the same league.
testimonial19: The game helped me learn Korean culture words without feeling like studying.
testimonial20: I shared the BLACKPINK link once and our chat turned it into a mini tournament.
testimonial21: The K-culture box reward feels broader and more fun than a single merch item.
testimonial22: I was not in one specific fandom, so K-culture Mix was the perfect default.
testimonial23: The fan challenge disclaimer made it clear this is unofficial, which I appreciated.
testimonial24: K-pop mode, drama words, and the Seoul prize hit all my interests at once.
testimonial25: My followers wanted their own fandom challenge link after I posted my result.
testimonial26: The leaderboard did not split by fandom, so switching themes did not ruin my progress.
testimonial27: I can now send the exact fandom link to the right group chat.
testimonial28: K-culture Mix words were easy to understand even for friends new to K-pop.
testimonial29: I got hooked trying to defend #1 after the 10-minute league refresh.
testimonial30: The fan-themed words made the game feel personal without needing official logos.
testimonial31: I tried (G)I-DLE mode first and ended up playing three more fandom packs.
testimonial32: StanBeat finally feels like a K-pop fan playground, not just one fandom challenge.

stdDevGaussianDesc: Width of bot distribution. Controls rank variance
hardCapDesc: • Minimum hard cap: 5,000ms (5s) — defense against unrealistic times
leagueSizeRangeDesc: Number of synthetic players per league (hash-deterministic)
maxPlayersDesc: Maximum players assigned via deterministic hash.
totalLeaguesCounterDesc: Distribution of "World X Leagues" visible number
totalLeaguesMeanDesc: Average number of leagues shown. Higher = more scale
totalLeaguesStdDesc: Random fluctuation per session. Too high = low trust
minFloorDesc: Distributed number never drops below this room
botOvertakeTimingDesc: Overtake interval when user is rank #1
overtakeGapMinDesc: Minimum gap when bot retakes #1. Lower = nail-biter finish
overtakeTargetDesc: • Occurs only when user maintains consecutive #1 rank
guestShowcaseWinnersDesc: Virtual winner distribution for logged-out home
baseOffsetDesc: Base offset of virtual records. Real mean = offset + 4000ms
showcaseLogicDesc: • Controls perceived competition level for new users
